meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Scale Your Development
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps for pennies. Master SaaS pricing strategies and leverage AI tools to scale your freelance business effectively. Read our case study.
content.answer_block: Moving beyond basic apps requires shifting from hourly billing to value-based SaaS pricing. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for complex logic, you can deliver enterprise-grade features in record time, justifying premium rates that transform your freelance operation into a high-margin software agency.
content.intro:
You’re stuck in the 'CRUD-app treadmill,' grinding out basic landing pages for clients who treat you like a commodity. You know you have the technical skill to build scalable SaaS products, but the gap between 'good code' and 'profitable product' feels like a canyon. 

It’s time to stop competing on price and start competing on value. By integrating automated scaffolding with high-velocity AI coding, you can condense weeks of development into days, allowing you to charge for the outcome, not just the hours spent typing.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Stop estimating hours. Identify the business metrics your client gains (e.g., lead conversion, time saved) and map these to three distinct SaaS pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing table schema for a B2B app that includes a 'Starter', 'Growth', and 'Enterprise' tier, focusing on seat-based and usage-based billing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe billing integration based on the generated pricing schema'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Engine
steps.description: Standardize your payment infrastructure. Use an abstraction layer to ensure you can swap payment providers without rewriting your core application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular billing service interface in TypeScript that handles subscription lifecycle events and invoice generation for Stripe.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'billing-service.ts' and link it to the existing user authentication flow

steps.title: Automate Feature Gating
steps.description: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) that automatically syncs with your pricing tiers to prevent feature leakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks user subscription status against a feature flag configuration before allowing route access.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'middleware.ts' to enforce pricing tier restrictions on all /dashboard routes

steps.title: Deploy Usage Tracking
steps.description: Capture the data points needed for usage-based pricing. If you can't measure it, you can't bill for it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a lightweight event logger that tracks API calls and storage usage per organization ID for billing reconciliation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a background job that aggregates daily usage metrics for the billing summary'

steps.title: Client-Facing Reporting
steps.description: Build a transparent dashboard so your client's customers can track their own usage and subscription costs, reducing support overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React dashboard component that visualizes real-time subscription usage against tier limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'UsageDashboard.tsx' and integrate with the backend billing API

pro_tips: Always build a 'Trial-to-Paid' funnel into the core architecture; retrofitting it later is a nightmare.
Use usage-based billing even for entry-level tiers to capture long-tail revenue as your client grows.
Automate your invoice reconciliation with a webhook-driven system to avoid manual bookkeeping.
Provide a 'White-label' option as a high-margin add-on; clients will pay 2x for custom branding.
Never hardcode pricing; pull it from a remote config or database so you can adjust rates without redeploying.
content.next_steps: Audit your current client project for potential value-based upgrades and implement the billing engine using the steps above.
content.conversion_block: Stop working harder and start working smarter by building products that pay you back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
